transition_record.writer_rule: Only the owning transition gate database role writes protected state; the authenticated request actor is recorded separately as cause and never becomes the database writer.
transition_record.atomicity: Protected state, ledger side effects, state version increment, and audit event commit in one PostgreSQL transaction or all roll back.
transition_record.replay_key: transition_request_id is unique within immutable scope and aggregate
transition_record.concurrency: expected_state_version must match and exactly one monotone new_state_version is committed

invariants.assertion: A reservation, debit, reversal, or release cannot produce a negative available or reserved balance.
An approval claim is scope-, payload-, decision-, and expiry-bound and can be consumed exactly once.
Conflicting claims or locks cannot both be held for the same protected resource and scope.
Budget, claims, and locks remain held while an external effect is possible, unknown, indeterminate, or awaiting compensation.
Execution attempt numbers are unique and strictly increasing per action intent.
Committed intent, dispatch journal, grant, bound local runtime fence, and attempt are durable before adapter invocation.
Failure to persist the audit event rolls back the protected transition and all ledger side effects.
Startup and restore remain not-ready until all blocking recovery markers have terminal evidence.
An unknown ledger status blocks quiescence and cannot authorize a transition, settlement, release, or readiness.

fencing.stage_1_local_runtime_fence: A durable current local executor epoch/token gates every new external effect. The grant and attempt retain the exact bound historical fence as immutable evidence.
fencing.recovery_context: Internal settlement, indeterminate, and reconciliation transitions require an authorized current recovery context but do not require the stale executor fence to become current.
fencing.stage_4_distributed_ownership_fence: Deferred to Stage 4 and not enabled or implied by the Stage 1 local runtime fence.

crash_rule: After persisted dispatch preparation, an attempt record alone cannot prove that adapter contact did not occur. Without authoritative no-effect evidence or successful registered reconciliation, recovery records an indeterminate effect and prohibits automatic retry.
